marketState: neutral, mild bullish tilt into fall on weather/positioning, capped by storage surplus
summary: Front price ($2.916) is trading almost exactly at model fair value ($2.923, FAIR status), so there is no near-term mispricing edge to lean on. Weather demand is running well above the 5-year normal in both the 1-7D and 8-16D windows (+19.3% / +17.8%, rangePos near the top of range) and rising, which is a genuine bullish input into a cooling-driven summer market. Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, a crowded, contrarian-bullish setup that historically precedes short-covering rallies, and the fair-value curve itself slopes up toward $3.06 by +21 days. Working against that: storage sits +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf surprise build, and price is running -14.4% YTD versus a -6.9% median (seasonality reliability flagged Low), so the surplus and weak YTD trend cap enthusiasm. Technicals are split by timeframe - bullish/EMA-bullish on 5m/15m but bearish on 4h/1d/1w - so there is no confirmed directional trend to trade right now.

longTerm.rationale: Cooling demand is running +18-19% above 5y normal and rising, managed money is net short at the 8th percentile (crowded short, contrarian bullish), and the fair-value curve rises from $2.92 to $3.06 over the next 3 weeks with September historically the strongest month. This offsets the +180 Bcf storage surplus and weak YTD seasonality, but that surplus is why conviction is medium rather than high.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $2.65, or storage surprises continuing to run bearish (bigger builds than forecast) while weather demand normalizes back toward the 5y average, would invalidate the long thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is sitting right on fair value (spread -$0.007, status FAIR) so there is no mispricing to trade, and technicals actively disagree by timeframe (bullish trend/EMA on 5m-15m vs. bearish on 4h-1d-1w). The near-dated futures curve is flat-to-slightly-backwardated (Sep/Oct near front), giving no contango/backwardation edge either.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a daily close above the 4h/1d EMA(50) with RSI reclaiming above 50 to confirm a bullish momentum shift toward $3.00+, or (b) a pullback into the $2.70-$2.75 zone to buy in line with the long-term thesis, or (c) a clean breakdown below $2.85 with daily RSI failing to hold the high-30s, which would favor a short scalp toward $2.70.

keyDrivers: Weather demand +18-19% above 5y normal in both 1-7D and 8-16D windows and trending up (+15.4 24h change)
Managed money net short at 8th percentile of 5y range - crowded short, contrarian bullish
Price at fair value now ($2.916 vs $2.923 fair) but fair value curve rises to $3.06 by +21 days
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf weekly surprise, capping upside
Technicals diverge by timeframe: bullish intraday (5m/15m) vs bearish daily/weekly trend
risks: Storage surplus could deepen if injections keep beating forecast, pressuring price lower despite hot weather demand
